title: Chicken Souvlaki
cScaleSummary: C2 because the cholesterol-contributing ingredients are the boneless skinless chicken thighs and prepared tzatziki. The lemon, garlic, salt, pepper, oregano, olive oil, onion, pita, and tomatoes contribute 0 mg cholesterol. Spread across 4 servings, the animal-derived ingredients place the recipe in the 26–75 mg cholesterol range per serving.
ldlActiveSummary: LDL-Active is false because the recipe is centered on chicken thighs and prepared tzatziki rather than a fiber-forward structure built around legumes, whole grains, vegetables, nuts, or seeds. It uses olive oil, lemon, garlic, oregano, onion, and tomatoes, but the overall dish is not actively LDL-supportive.
intro: Souvlaki is one of the oldest street foods in Greece. The word simply means “little skewer.” Small pieces of meat are marinated with lemon, olive oil, garlic, and oregano, then grilled over very hot coals. The dish is served either wrapped in pita or plated with vegetables and tzatziki. The defining flavors are bright lemon, aromatic oregano, and aggressive grilling. Proper souvlaki should have crisp charred edges while remaining juicy inside.

items.prep: minced into a paste with kosher salt

stepGroups.title: Marinate the Chicken
items: Place the lemon zest ({{quantity:lemon-zest}}), garlic paste ({{quantity:garlic}}), kosher salt ({{quantity:kosher-salt-marinade}}), black pepper ({{quantity:black-pepper}}), and chopped oregano ({{quantity:fresh-oregano}}) in a large bowl. Add the olive oil ({{quantity:olive-oil-marinade}}) and lemon juice ({{quantity:lemon-juice-marinade}}) and whisk to form a loose marinade. Add the chicken pieces ({{quantity:chicken-thighs}}) and mix thoroughly so every piece is coated. Cover and refrigerate for about 2 hours. Do not marinate longer than 4 hours or the lemon will begin tightening the meat.

stepGroups.title: Prepare the Onion Lemon Salad
items: Place the sliced red onion ({{quantity:red-onion}}) in a bowl. Sprinkle with the kosher salt ({{quantity:kosher-salt-onion-salad}}) and lemon juice ({{quantity:lemon-juice-onion-salad}}). Toss well and let sit at room temperature for about 10 minutes until the onion softens.

stepGroups.title: Assemble the Souvlaki
items: Thread the chicken onto metal skewers or soaked wooden skewers, packing the pieces fairly close together so they just touch. This slows moisture loss and helps the chicken stay juicy while grilling.

stepGroups.title: Grill the Souvlaki
items: Preheat a grill to very high heat. Place the skewers directly over the hottest part of the grill and cook about 8 to 10 minutes total, rotating frequently so several sides develop char. During the last minute of grilling, lightly brush the skewers with a little of the marinade remaining in the bowl to refresh the lemon and oregano aroma.

stepGroups.title: Grill the Pita
items: Lightly brush the pita with olive oil and grill briefly until warm and lightly charred.

stepGroups.title: Wrap and Serve
items: Remove the chicken from the skewers. Finish the chicken with a small pinch of kosher salt. Lay out the warm pita ({{quantity:pita-breads}}) and spread each with tzatziki ({{quantity:prepared-tzatziki}}). Divide the chicken among them, then add the onion lemon salad and tomato wedges ({{quantity:tomatoes}}). Fold each pita around the filling, keeping the filling centered and compact so it eats cleanly out of the hand.
